candidates.why: Explicit total revenue line; clearly the company’s top-line sales figure.
Finance income is non-operating and not the primary revenue from operations; used only if no operating revenue line existed, which is not the case here.
Component of finance income, not operating revenue.
Interest on deposits; financial income, not operating revenue.
Explicitly non-operating; includes various finance items, not core revenue.

candidates.row_label: Total comprehensive income for the year
candidates.why: A bottom-line comprehensive income measure, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Total comprehensive income for the period
candidates.why: Comprehensive income, not revenue.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive Income before Minority Interest - Total
candidates.why: Comprehensive income metric, not top-line revenue.

variables.notes: ‘Total Revenue’ is the clear top-line revenue figure and should be used as REVT; no need to combine with any other rows.

candidates.why: This is a contra to R&D (government grants), not a direct cost of sales. It is negative and classified with R&D, not as cost of goods sold.
An operating expense bucket, likely SG&A, not a distinct cost of goods sold line. The statement appears to be for a pre-commercial/R&D company without separate COGS.
A specific operating expense, part of overhead, not cost of goods sold.
Financing result, not COGS.
Net financial result, not related to production or services.
Finance costs, not direct costs of sales.
Lease-related expense; overhead/financing-like, not traditional COGS.
Balance sheet asset; not an income statement expense line.
Balance sheet liability; not COGS itself.

variables.notes: The income statement presents expenses as R&D, payroll, depreciation, and ‘Other operating expenses’, with no line resembling a distinct cost of goods sold or cost of sales. The company appears to be R&D-focused with negligible revenues, so COGS is effectively not separately reported. Per instructions, when the concept truly does not exist as a separate line, COGS is set to an empty list.

candidates.why: This is the main operating overhead bucket (non-R&D, non-depreciation). It likely aggregates selling, general, and administrative costs and fits SG&A best among available lines.

variables.notes: No explicit SG&A line is provided; ‘Other operating expenses’ is the best available proxy for SG&A components and should be used as a single bucket per the hierarchy rules.

candidates.why: This appears to be government grants related to R&D and is recorded as a negative amount, i.e., a reduction of R&D cost, not the gross R&D expense itself.
Explicitly labeled as R&D-related consulting costs; a direct R&D expense component and thus an appropriate line for XRD.

variables.notes: ‘Consultants R&D projects’ is a clear R&D expense component. The ‘Research & Development Expense - Government Grants’ line is a contra/grant and not an expense, so it is excluded from XRD.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense - Net of Capitalized Interest
candidates.why: Primary explicit interest expense line (non-lease), best match for interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
candidates.why: Interest portion of lease liabilities; a component of interest expense that may be reported separately.
Net interest result; useful fallback if separate interest expense lines are missing for some years.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental
candidates.why: Supplemental disclosure of lease interest; secondary fallback for some periods.
Interest income, not expense; should not be used for XINT unless only net interest were available, which is not the case here.
Interest income on deposits; not interest expense.

candidates.row_label: Interest Expense - Net of Capitalized Interest - Balancing value
candidates.why: Balancing adjustment row, not a primary operating line; only a last-resort fallback, if at all.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive Income before Minority Interest - Total
candidates.why: Comprehensive income subtotal, not an interest line.

final_choice.row_label: Interest Expense - Net of Capitalized Interest
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities
Interest Expense on Financial Lease Liabilities - Supplemental

variables.notes: The priority list first uses the main interest expense line, then lease interest, then its supplemental disclosure, and finally the net interest line as a fallback. Interest income rows are intentionally excluded from XINT.

candidates.why: Represents shareholders’ equity; appears to be equity attributable to owners. Preferred over ‘Total Equity’ when both are present to avoid potential double counting with any (hypothetical) non-controlling interests.
Total equity figure, numerically almost identical to ‘Equity’ but labeled as a total. Used only if no separate owner-equity line existed.
Technical balancing row, not the substantive equity measure to use as BE.
Balancing item, not the primary equity figure.

candidates.row_label: Balancing Item - Total Liability and Equity
candidates.why: Balancing row for the full balance sheet, not equity itself.
Total of equity and liabilities; not pure equity.

variables.notes: ‘Equity’ is taken as book equity attributable to shareholders. ‘Total Equity’ is a near-duplicate total and is not used to avoid ambiguity. No separate non-controlling interest line is present.

candidates.row_label: Comprehensive Income before Minority Interest - Total
candidates.why: Income statement subtotal, not a balance sheet minority interest line. It does not represent non-controlling interests in equity.

variables.notes: There is no balance sheet line for non-controlling interests or minority interest. Therefore MIB is set to an empty list (treated as zero), consistent with the instruction to avoid using ‘Total Equity’ when MIB is unavailable.
